title: Welcome to Malta — Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Hey, and welcome to Malta! 👋 This one's just for fun — a taste of island life and your very first Maltese words.
No pressure, no grammar drills. Three little tours: the islands, the food, and the way people talk. Let's go!
🏝️ PART 1 — THE ISLANDS 🏝️
Malta is actually three inhabited islands. The big one — Malta. The greener, slower one:
That's Gozo. And the tiny one between them, famous for its blue lagoon:
Comino. Two words you'll see and hear constantly — the sea:
And the sun, which Malta has plenty of:
🥟 PART 2 — TASTES OF MALTA 🥟
If you try one thing, make it this — a warm, flaky pastry filled with ricotta or peas:
Wash it down with Malta's bright orange, love-it-or-hate-it soft drink:
And when something tastes amazing, the word you want is:
Delicious. 😋
💬 PART 3 — SOUNDS LIKE MALTA 💬
Maltese conversation is full of tiny words. The most Maltese one of all means so, well, of course — everything and nothing:
Drop a 'Mela' into a chat and you'll instantly sound local. To get someone's attention:
Listen — or 'hey'. And a warm word for someone you like:
Darling, or dear. ❤️
🇲🇹 GOOD TO KNOW 🇲🇹
Two quick things. One: Malta has two official languages, Maltese and English — so you'll always get by, and a few Maltese words go a long way.
Two: Maltese is the only Semitic language written in our alphabet — Arabic roots, with Italian, English and French stirred in. That's why 'good morning' is borrowed from French:
✅ That's it!
That's your welcome to Malta. When you're ready for the real thing, Lesson 1 of the course starts with greetings and the alphabet. Saħħa! 🇲🇹
